steps.action: POST /v1/completions with echo:true, prompt 'The capital of France is', max_tokens:5
steps.expected: HTTP 200 with choices[0].text starting with the prompt
steps.observed: HTTP 200 returned. choices[0].text = 'The capital of France is<|channel>thought\n<channel|>Paris'. Text starts with 'The capital of France is' — confirmed.

assertions.title: Prompt logprobs appear in response when echo: true

steps.action: POST /v1/completions with echo:true, logprobs:true, prompt 'The capital of France is', max_tokens:5
steps.expected: Logprobs array length >= prompt token count (27). Prompt tokens appear before completion tokens.
steps.observed: Logprobs array has only 9 tokens. usage.prompt_tokens=27 but logprobs.tokens has 9 entries. The prompt text 'The capital of France is' does NOT appear in any logprobs token. Tokens are: ['<|turn>', 'model', '\n', '<|channel>', 'thought', '\n', '<channel|>', 'Paris', '<turn|>'] — all are template suffix + generated tokens. No user prompt tokens present.
All 5 requests show identical pattern: 9 logprobs tokens, none containing the user prompt. Consistent failure.

assertions.issues: Logprobs array only contains 9 tokens (template suffix + generated), missing all 27 prompt tokens. The echo parameter correctly affects choices[0].text (prompt text is prepended), but logprobs are not computed for the prompt portion. The logprobs tokens start from '<|turn>model\n' (the model turn start) and include only generated content after that. No user prompt tokens ('The', ' capital', ' of', ' France', ' is') appear in the logprobs array.

steps.action: POST /v1/completions with echo:true, logprobs:true, 5 separate requests
steps.expected: token_logprobs[0] is JSON null in all requests
steps.observed: token_logprobs[0] = null in all 5 requests. Token at position 0 is consistently '<|turn>' (Gemma template token).

assertions.title: Prompt + completion logprobs are concatenated correctly

steps.action: POST /v1/completions with echo:true, logprobs:true — check token counts
steps.observed: len(logprobs.tokens) = 9, expected = 32. Token count mismatch. The logprobs array does not include all prompt tokens.

steps.action: Check prompt boundary in logprobs tokens
steps.expected: First N tokens = prompt tokens in order, remaining = generated tokens in order
steps.observed: Logprobs tokens: ['<|turn>', 'model', '\n', '<|channel>', 'thought', '\n', '<channel|>', 'Paris', '<turn|>']. None of these tokens correspond to the user prompt 'The capital of France is'. The first 3 tokens are Gemma template suffix tokens (model turn start), the remaining 6 are generated content (thinking channel + answer + turn end). No prompt boundary is identifiable.
All 5 requests: logprobs_tokens=9, prompt_tokens=27, completion_tokens=5. Consistent mismatch.

assertions.issues: The logprobs array does not include prompt tokens. Only 9 of the expected 32 tokens are present. The array contains only the model's turn start template tokens + generated tokens. The user prompt tokens are completely absent from the logprobs, making it impossible to verify correct concatenation of prompt + completion logprobs. This is the same root cause as VAL-ECHO-002: echo logprobs are not being computed for the prompt portion.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 2 passed, 2 failed. VAL-ECHO-001 (echo parameter accepted) and VAL-ECHO-004 (position 0 null logprob) pass. VAL-ECHO-002 (prompt logprobs with echo) and VAL-ECHO-005 (concatenation ordering) fail because the logprobs array only contains 9 tokens (template suffix + generated) instead of the expected 32 (27 prompt + 5 completion). The echo feature correctly prepends prompt text to choices[0].text, but prompt tokens are not included in the logprobs array.
